records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Materialized (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Materializing (?).]

defs: To invest with material characteristics; to make perceptible to the senses; hence, to present to the mind through the medium of material objects.
quotes: Having with wonderful art and beauty materialized, if I may so call it, a scheme of abstracted notions, and clothed the most nice, refined conceptions of philosophy in sensible images.

defs: To regard as matter; to consider or explain by the laws or principles which are appropriate to matter.
To cause to assume a character appropriate to material things; to occupy with material interests; as, to materialize thought.
To make visible in, or as in, a material form; -- said of spirits.
quotes: A female spirit form temporarily materialized, and not distinguishable from a human being.

defs: To appear as a material form; to take substantial shape.
To come into existence; as, the promised donations never materialized.
